query: Bill Cassidy Louisiana Senate primary 2026 endorsement runoff Letlow Fleming
answer:
## 2026 Louisiana Senate Race: Bill Cassidy's Seat — Letlow vs. Fleming Runoff

### Key Finding: Cassidy Defeated; Runoff Set for June 27, 2026

Bill Cassidy, the incumbent Republican senator from Louisiana, **ran for reelection but was eliminated in the Republican primary on May 16, 2026**, finishing third with just 24.8% of the vote. His 2021 vote to convict Donald Trump in the second impeachment trial was the primary factor behind his defeat. He is the first elected incumbent U.S. senator to lose renomination since Richard Lugar (R-IN) in 2012.

---

### The Runoff Candidates

**Julia Letlow (R) — Primary Winner (44.8%, 179,876 votes)**
- U.S. Representative from Louisiana's 5th Congressional District (since 2021)
- First Republican woman elected to Congress from Louisiana
- Won a 2021 special election after her husband Luke Letlow died from COVID-19 before taking office
- Holds a Ph.D. in communications; authored the Parents Bill of Rights Act
- **Endorsed by Donald Trump on January 17, 2026** — the most significant endorsement in the race

**John Fleming (R) — Primary Runner-Up (28.3%, 113,428 votes)**
- Louisiana State Treasurer (since 2024)
- Former U.S. Representative from LA-4 (2009–2017); physician and Navy veteran
- Served in the Trump administration (multiple roles including White House aide)
- Previously ran for Senate in 2016 (finished 5th)
- Accused the Letlow campaign of offering him a CDC position to drop out (denied by Letlow's camp)

---

### Primary/Runoff System

Louisiana **eliminated its traditional "jungle primary"** (nonpartisan blanket primary) in January 2024 and restored a partisan primary system. Since no candidate cleared 50%, a **Republican runoff is scheduled for June 27, 2026**. The runoff winner faces the Democratic nominee on November 3, 2026. Given Louisiana's strong Republican lean, the runoff winner is the heavy favorite in November.

---

### Polling (Runoff: Letlow vs. Fleming)

| Pollster | Dates | Letlow | Fleming |
|----------|-------|--------|---------|
| Quantus Insights | May 6–7, 2026 | **45%** | 40% |
| BDPC | April 28–30, 2026 | **42%** | 31% |

Letlow leads in both available runoff polls, though the margin could tighten (5 points in the most recent poll).

---

### Fundraising (as of May 17, 2026)

| Candidate | Total Raised | Total Spent | Cash on Hand |
|-----------|-------------|-------------|--------------|
| John Fleming | $11,286,639 | $9,897,447 | $1,389,264 |
| Julia Letlow | $4,398,631 | $2,785,529 | $1,613,102 |

Fleming outraised Letlow nearly 3-to-1 but spent heavily. Letlow enters the runoff with a slight cash-on-hand advantage.

---

### Key Dates
- **June 27, 2026** — Republican runoff (Letlow vs. Fleming)
- **November 3, 2026** — General election

---

**Data Note:** Information sourced primarily from Wikipedia (accessed May 18, 2026). Live web search was unavailable, so developments after May 18 could not be confirmed.

key_facts: Bill Cassidy ran for reelection but was eliminated in the May 16, 2026 Republican primary, finishing third with 24.8% (99,479 votes) — the first elected incumbent senator to lose renomination since Richard Lugar in 2012.
Julia Letlow won the primary with 44.8% (179,876 votes); she is a U.S. Representative from LA-5 and was endorsed by Donald Trump on January 17, 2026.
John Fleming finished second with 28.3% (113,428 votes); he is the Louisiana State Treasurer and former congressman who served in the Trump administration.
A Republican runoff between Letlow and Fleming is scheduled for June 27, 2026, since no candidate exceeded 50%.
Louisiana eliminated its jungle primary system in January 2024 and restored partisan primaries; this is the first Senate race under the new system.
Letlow leads Fleming in both available runoff polls: 45-40 (Quantus Insights, May 6-7) and 42-31 (BDPC, April 28-30).
Fleming outraised Letlow nearly 3-to-1 ($11.3M vs $4.4M) but Letlow has a slight cash-on-hand advantage entering the runoff ($1.61M vs $1.39M).
Cassidy's defeat was driven primarily by his 2021 vote to convict Trump in the second impeachment trial.
Fleming accused the Letlow campaign of offering him a CDC position to drop out; the Letlow campaign denied the allegation.
The general election is November 3, 2026; the Republican runoff winner will be the strong favorite in heavily Republican Louisiana.
